question_text: Two identical metal spheres are brought close together. Sphere A has a charge of +8 units and Sphere B has a charge of -6 units. They touch briefly, then are separated. What is the final charge on Sphere A after separation?
explanation: When two identical conductors touch, charge distributes equally. Total charge = +8 + (-6) = +2 units. This divides equally: +2 ÷ 2 = +1 unit per sphere. This requires understanding charge conservation and equal distribution in identical conductors.
